title: Prior Art: The Complete Innovation Timeline With Cryptographic Proof
excerpt: Every innovation in the RAR ecosystem — timestamped, commit-hashed, and publicly verifiable. Single-file agents, trading card identity, three universal faces, the forge, deck hotloading, federation, and 10 more. If someone says they built it too, show them the commits.

title: Foundation: The Single File Principle and Why It Changes Everything
excerpt: How one constraint — every agent is one .py file — became the foundation for an entire ecosystem. The manifest, the registry, the store, and the zero-install web app all flow from this single decision.

title: Three Faces: Making Agent Cards Universal
excerpt: Every agent card has three renderings — Icon, Full Art, and ASCII — so it can exist anywhere in space and time, virtual or real. From terminal to trading card to holographic display.

title: The Simplicity Audit: Killing Five Systems to Ship One
excerpt: We removed tier gating, wave unlocks, intent toggles, mode dials, and progressive disclosure. The result: every feature visible to every user with zero concepts to learn.

title: The Forge: Emergent Identity From Agent x Owner x Time
excerpt: The companion card forge system fuses three inputs you can't control — the agent's hash, your identity, and the current 15-minute time epoch — into a unique card that shifts over time.

title: Killing Every Emoji: Why SVG Glyphs Beat Unicode Art
excerpt: We found 87 emoji usages across the codebase. We replaced every one with hand-crafted SVG glyphs from the same art system that renders trading cards. The visual identity is now unified.

title: The .py.card Format: Shedding and Re-Shelling Agent Identity
excerpt: An agent can exist as a bare .py (just code) or a .py.card (code + trading card shell). Strip the shell, the agent works. Add it back from the registry, the card returns. One file, two modes.

title: Hatching: What It Means for a Card to Start Its Public Life
excerpt: A card isn't alive until it's hatched — deployed to the global network with all three faces rendering, all metadata valid, all 10 requirements met. This is the definition of production-ready.

title: The Deck Extension: .py.card.DeckName and Runtime Hotloading
excerpt: The file extension chain encodes the full packaging hierarchy. Strip layers to simplify, add layers to package. Decks hotload into the runtime like models — switch decks, switch agents, no restart.
